summary: Matching-oriented pass over the mnDiagram menu/stat screens. The PR reworked main diagram input/navigation, popup animation/text placement, stat-detail screens, and static data headers using typed overlays instead of raw address arithmetic. decomp.dev reported +184 matched bytes, one new full match for `mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc`, and major unmatched-item improvements, especially `fn_802461BC` and `mnDiagram_InputProc`; it also reported one broken match in `mnDiagram3_80247008`.

key_files.evidence: +852/-233; added `mnDiagram_AnimTable`, rewrote `mnDiagram_InputProc`, improved popup/cursor/header code, and bot reported `mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc` at 100%.
+259/-88; added `fn_802461BC`, fake JObj translate inlines, typed table access, and detail navigation cleanup.
+40/-50; switched to `HSD_ASSERT`, typed anim loop access, and cleaned user_data allocation/row spacing code.
Added `mnDiagram3_PosTable` and `mnDiagram3_StatTable`; converted raw `u8` extern blobs to typed structs.
Changed `mnDiagram2_803EEB60` to `AnimLoopSettings[2]` and removed unused assert string externs.

what_changed: Added a typed `mnDiagram_AnimTable` overlay over `mnDiagram_803EE728` so popup/cursor code can access `Point3d` entries and trailing `AnimLoopSettings` fields at fixed offsets without raw `char*` arithmetic.

Expanded `mnDiagram_InputProc` from simple placeholder-like D-pad handling into detailed fighter/name-mode navigation: clamps `mn_804A04F0.hovered_selection`, skips locked fighters and null name entries, updates scroll arrows, routes to popups and detail screens, and stores cursor state in `gmMainLib_8015CC34()`.

Improved sorted fighter/name computations in `mnDiagram_8023FA6C` and `mnDiagram_8023FC28` with explicit `u8` casts, typed `mnDiagram_Assets*` access, adjusted stack padding, and expanded comparison predicates.

Reworked popup creation/animation functions (`mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc`, `mnDiagram_80240D94`, `mnDiagram_80241310`) to use typed table fields, scoped float temporaries, `HSD_ASSERTREPORT`, and explicit text/user_data initialization.

Changed grid header/icon creation (`mnDiagram_802427B4`, `mnDiagram_80242C0C`) to traverse `sorted_names` and `sorted_fighters` while skipping invalid entries, rather than directly indexing visible rows/columns.
Converted several direct `jobj->translate` reads to `HSD_JObjGetTranslationX/Y/Z` and several direct writes to setter-style calls or local fake setter inlines to match codegen.

Added `fn_802461BC` in `mndiagram3.c`, implementing input handling for the ranked/stat detail screen: back/menu transitions, mode toggling, popup movement, scroll offset changes, row label rebuilding, and detail refresh calls.
Introduced `mnDiagram3_PosTable` and `mnDiagram3_StatTable` in `mndiagram3.static.h`, giving names to position vectors and stat-index data previously represented as raw byte arrays.
Replaced manual `OSReport`/`__assert` string patterns in touched code with `HSD_ASSERT` or `HSD_ASSERTREPORT`, requiring `baselib/debug.h` includes.
smart_moves: Used overlay structs with explicit padding (`pad_24`, `pad_4C`, `x40`, `x58`, `x64`) to preserve exact data offsets while making call sites more type-safe and more matchable.
Kept hard-to-name data fields address/offset-based rather than inventing semantics, e.g. `x40`, `x58`, `x64`, `x0`, `xC`, `x18`, and `indices`.

Mirrored original control flow in `mnDiagram_InputProc` with labels such as `nc_outer`, `dn_n_find`, `rt_f_inner`, and sentinel values `0x19`/`0x78`, which is often necessary for menu traversal matches.
Used explicit `u8` casts for persistent data indexes (`fighter_kos[(u8) j]`, `vs_kos[(u8) arg1]`) where the original code likely used byte-sized IDs.

Created local fake `HSD_JObjSetTranslateX/Y/Z` inlines under `#define __FILE__ "jobj.h"` with specific assert line numbers, then reset `__FILE__`, to match inlined JObj setter behavior without changing global headers.
Replaced raw base-pointer arithmetic with typed `mnDiagram_Assets* assets` access for `sorted_fighters`, `sorted_names`, and archive data, making later decomp work easier to audit.
Used decomp.dev feedback to target high-value unmatched functions: the bot reported `fn_802461BC` from 0.00% to 85.31% and `mnDiagram_InputProc` from 22.71% to 89.53%.
decomp_lessons: When a static data symbol is really a blob containing vectors, animation-loop settings, strings, or tables, introduce an overlay struct with padding instead of leaving repeated `(char*) base + offset` casts.

For menu grids that display filtered lists, matching code may need to reproduce original skip loops over sorted arrays, not just index `base + visible_index`; preserve locked-fighter and null-name checks.
Small integer casts matter in persistent-stat code: fighter/name IDs stored as bytes should often be cast to `u8` before array indexing.
Standard project assertion helpers (`HSD_ASSERT`, `HSD_ASSERTREPORT`) can remove ad-hoc string externs and produce better matches when line numbers and strings line up.
Direct JObj field access versus `HSD_JObjGetTranslation*` or inlined setter behavior changes codegen; choose the form that matches the original, even if that means a local fake inline.
Stack padding remains a matching lever: this PR changed examples like `PAD_STACK(8)` to `PAD_STACK(16)` and `mnDiagram_CursorProc` to `PAD_STACK(8)`.
decomp.dev match reports should be checked for regressions as well as wins; this PR gained a full `mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc` match but broke `mnDiagram3_80247008` from 100% to 97.22%.
naming_conventions: `mnDiagram_AnimTable` names a typed overlay for address-backed static data while retaining offset-like fields for unknown members.
`mnDiagram3_PosTable` and `mnDiagram3_StatTable` use module prefixes and describe the layout role without over-naming unknown trailing bytes.
Padding fields use offset-based names such as `pad_24` and `pad_4C`; unknown struct members use `x40`, `x58`, `x64`, `x50`, `x68`, `x78`.
Locals like `assets` and `user_data` clarify former raw pointer arithmetic without changing public symbol names.
Address-style function names such as `fn_802461BC`, `mnDiagram_80241310`, and `mnDiagram3_8024714C` were retained where no semantic name was evidenced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Typed overlays over raw data blobs: `mnDiagram_AnimTable`, `mnDiagram3_PosTable`, `mnDiagram3_StatTable`, and `AnimLoopSettings mnDiagram2_803EEB60[2]`.
Goto-shaped loops and explicit labels in `mnDiagram_InputProc` to mirror original branch structure.
Sentinel IDs `0x19` for fighters and `0x78` for names when filtered traversal runs off the table.
Explicit `PAD_STACK` tuning in sorting, popup, input, and cursor functions.
Scoped float temporaries (`y`, `z`, `new_var`) around text-position assignments to influence load/store order.
Use of `HSD_ASSERTREPORT(line, ptr, "Can't get user_data\n")` and `HSD_ASSERT(line, jobj)` instead of manual `OSReport`/`__assert` blocks.
Local fake JObj translate setters with `#undef __FILE__`, `#define __FILE__ "jobj.h"`, exact assert lines 932/947/962, and `ftCo_800C6AFC` dirty handling.
Repeated count recomputation and inline unlocked-fighter loops where helper calls such as `mnDiagram_CountUnlockedFighters()` did not match.
review_feedback: No human review comments or PR body text were present in the slice; evidence is from the diff and decomp.dev bot report.
decomp.dev reported matched code 69.13% with +184 bytes.
decomp.dev reported one new full match: `main/melee/mn/mndiagram` `mnDiagram_PopupAnimProc`, 74.29% to 100.00%, +131 bytes.

decomp.dev reported major unmatched improvements including `fn_802461BC` 0.00% to 85.31%, `mnDiagram_InputProc` 22.71% to 89.53%, `mnDiagram_80242C0C` 60.00% to 92.70%, and `mnDiagram_802427B4` 35.50% to 81.61%.
decomp.dev also reported one broken match: `main/melee/mn/mndiagram3` `mnDiagram3_80247008`, 100.00% to 97.22%, plus several unmatched regressions in `mndiagram2` and `mndiagram3`.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that restore or improve `mnDiagram3_80247008` after the 97.22% regression.
Search for later semantic naming of `fn_802461BC` and the `mnDiagram3` ranked-stat input proc.
Search for reuse of the fake `HSD_JObjSetTranslateX_Fake` / `__FILE__ "jobj.h"` matching tactic.
Find PRs that further type or rename `mnDiagram_AnimTable` fields `x40`, `x58`, and `x64`.
Find later work on `mnDiagram2_CreateStatRow` and `mnDiagram2_UpdateScrollArrows` regressions from this bot report.
Search for additional `mnDiagram_Assets.sorted_names` / `sorted_fighters` filtering loop cleanups.
